follow_ups.title: Shell injection via ctx.branch/rid in datum-closeout.ts
follow_ups.description: LLM-controlled values ctx.branch and rid are interpolated directly into shell command strings in datum-closeout.ts line 57. An adversarial agent response could inject commands via branch name (e.g. 'x; rm -rf .'). Also affects git tag and EPIC_DIR variable.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: Validate ctx.branch and rid against /^[a-zA-Z0-9._/-]+$/ before interpolation. Pass values as positional CLI args, not embedded shell strings.

follow_ups.title: Prompt injection via preamble content in datum-awake.ts
follow_ups.description: LLM-generated preamble content is interpolated into agent instruction strings containing embedded shell commands. Adversarial repo content (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md) could inject shell commands via the distill agent output.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: Never interpolate raw LLM output into agent instruction strings containing shell commands. Write preamble via a dedicated file-write tool call, then issue git commit in a separate agent with no LLM-controlled content in the prompt.

follow_ups.title: datum-go batch partitioning ignores DAG wave boundaries
follow_ups.description: Batch partitioning uses sequential index slicing into topological_order (MAX_BATCH=5). A task depending on a task in the previous wave can be sliced into the same batch, then await a depPromise that never resolves (the dep is in a later batch), causing a hang.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: Partition by buildWaves() output rather than sequential index. Each wave becomes one batch, or fill batches respecting wave boundaries so intra-batch deps are always resolvable.

follow_ups.title: Act batch loop inlined in datum-go.ts — should be in shared/utils.ts
follow_ups.description: The act phase batch coordination loop (lines 123-202) is inlined in datum-go.ts. Any change to batching strategy requires modifying datum-go rather than replacing a utility. Creates 1:1 coupling between orchestrator and act coordination logic.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: Extract entire batch loop to a pure function in shared/utils.ts. Call it from datum-go. Preserves no-nesting-limit intent while decoupling orchestration from batch coordination.

follow_ups.title: gate_validate passes silently when test signal file is missing
follow_ups.description: datum/gate.py line 740: the validate gate only checks last-test-signal.json if the file exists. When it doesn't exist, the gate passes silently — tests have never been run but the gate greens. Violates the gate's stated purpose.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: When signal_path does not exist and not yolo, call fail('No test signal found — run tests before validate gate'). Only skip this check in yolo mode.

follow_ups.title: list_sub_issues unchecked non-array API response
follow_ups.description: datum/github_issues.py line 218: json.loads(raw) is not guarded against a non-array response (e.g. pagination metadata object or error body). If the GH sub_issues REST endpoint returns an object, `for issue in issues` silently iterates over dict keys and crashes on issue['number'].
follow_ups.suggested_fix: After json.loads, assert isinstance(issues, list) and raise a descriptive RuntimeError if not.

follow_ups.title: build_lane_plan_from_epic silently emits dangling depends_on IDs
follow_ups.description: datum/github_issues.py line 247: depends_on_ids is built from raw int metadata without verifying that each referenced issue number corresponds to an actual lane in the plan. Dangling depends_on IDs are emitted silently, causing buildWaves() to throw at runtime.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: After building all lanes, second-pass validate every depends_on ID exists as a key in lanes. Raise ValueError listing unresolved references before returning.

follow_ups.title: update_issue_stage has two separate if stage == 'done' blocks
follow_ups.description: datum/github_issues.py line 306: two separate `if stage == 'done':` blocks handle comment (line 304) and close (line 306) separately. If the comment call raises, the issue is never closed. Two side-effects should be in one block.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: Merge both `if stage == 'done':` blocks into one. Put comment call and close call in the same block.

follow_ups.title: Lane type missing task_id field — identity only implicit in Record key
follow_ups.description: skills/src/shared/types.ts line 62: LanePlan.lanes is Record<string, Lane> but Lane does not include task_id. Lane identity is implicit. Downstream code must reach into lanePlan to look up lane by task_id, creating an inverted dependency.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: Add task_id: string field to the Lane interface. Update buildPacket() and all Lane accessors.

follow_ups.title: O(n*m) dependency filtering in datum-tdd-act-lane.ts
follow_ups.description: skills/src/datum-tdd-act-lane.ts lines 447 and 457: crossBatchFailed and in-batch dep filtering use filter() with nested includes() calls on arrays. For large lane plans this is O(n*m).
follow_ups.suggested_fix: Convert batchLaneIds and priorFailures to Set for O(1) lookups. Use has() instead of includes().

follow_ups.title: Closeout-data.json was not written by pipeline for run 20260614-132742
follow_ups.description: The run directory .datum/runs/20260614-132742/ was created but left empty — closeout-data.json was never written. This means pipeline telemetry is blind for this session. Likely caused by the pipeline not reaching the closeout phase.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: Ensure datum-closeout.ts always writes closeout-data.json even on partial runs. Write a minimal skeleton at run start and update fields as phases complete.

follow_ups.title: issue_to_number in build_lane_plan_from_epic is dead code
follow_ups.description: datum/github_issues.py line 236: issue_to_number dict is populated but never read. Could mislead readers into thinking dependency validation is performed via this map.
follow_ups.suggested_fix: Remove issue_to_number or wire it into the depends_on validation proposed in fu-007 (CORR-002).
